body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: My overall experience in the Journalism program at the School of Communication during this academic year has been moderate, with meaningful highs and some notable lows that I believe the institution has both the capacity and responsibility to address. The academic content, when delivered well, is genuinely rigorous and relevant, and I have formed valuable relationships with peers and some excellent faculty members. However, structural issues — inconsistent teaching quality, administrative inefficiencies, and gaps in student support — have at times undermined what could otherwise be an exceptional educational experience. With targeted improvements and a genuine commitment to acting on student feedback, this institution has the foundations to become truly outstanding.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Communication has been impressive and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I recognize the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The Journalism curriculum at the School of Communication appears to lag behind current industry standards in several significant areas that I believe require urgent review and revision. Many courses continue to teach outdated tools and methodologies that are rarely used in professional settings today, leaving students underprepared for actual workplace demands. The curriculum also lacks sufficient elective depth and specialization pathways, limiting students' ability to develop expertise in niche areas of growing importance. I raise concerns about the Academic Affairs Office and school leadership to conduct a thorough curriculum audit in close consultation with industry professionals and alumni to ensure course content remains relevant, rigorous, and career-oriented.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: Extracurricular activities at the School of Communication offer a reasonable range of options that serve the interests of a portion of the student body but do not yet reflect the full diversity of student interests and backgrounds. Core clubs and major university events are reasonably well-organized and attended, but support for emerging student initiatives is limited and the process for establishing new clubs is unnecessarily bureaucratic. Increasing the budget allocated to student activities, streamlining club registration procedures, and investing in purpose-built student activity spaces would encourage broader participation and strengthen the campus culture for the benefit of all enrolled students.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: Career preparation services at the School of Communication, offered through the Career Center, are reasonable and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: Financial services at the School of Communication, managed by the Finance Department, operate at a satisfactory level that fulfills basic requirements without particular distinction in either efficiency or student-centeredness. Tuition fee management and billing are generally accurate, though occasional discrepancies require follow-up that can be time-consuming. Scholarship and financial aid communication could be significantly improved — students often report uncertainty about disbursement timelines and eligibility criteria that creates unnecessary anxiety during critical financial planning periods. Greater transparency through automated notifications, a clearer scholarship portal, and improved staff responsiveness to financial queries would substantially raise the standard of financial services for the student community.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure at the School of Communication, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a acceptable level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: The learning resources and materials provided for my Journalism courses at the School of Communication are superb and represent a significant strength of the program. Course packs are comprehensive, well-organized, and current, incorporating recent academic publications, industry white papers, and multimedia content that enriches the learning experience beyond traditional textbooks. The university's digital library provides broad access to peer-reviewed journals and e-books, and the Learning Management System is intuitive, well-maintained, and effectively used by lecturers to share materials in a timely manner. I also applaud the provision of recorded lectures, which allows students to revisit complex material at their own pace — a genuinely valuable academic resource.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: The academic administrative processes at the School of Communication, overseen by the Academic Affairs Office, have been a source of considerable frustration for many students this semester. Course registration was plagued by system errors and conflicting timetables that took weeks to resolve, causing students to miss early enrollment advantages. Grade dispute processes are opaque and slow, with students waiting over a month without updates or acknowledgment of their submissions. Communication from administrative offices is reactive rather than proactive, and staff sometimes provide inconsistent information depending on who is contacted. I highlight a comprehensive review of administrative workflows, with investment in both system improvements and staff training as a matter of urgency.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: I have very few concerns to raise about my overall experience in the Journalism program at the School of Communication, and I genuinely believe this institution provides an impressive educational environment that prepares students well for their future careers. If I were to recommend any improvement, I would suggest further expanding industry partnership opportunities — additional mentorship programs, sponsored capstone projects, and guest lecture series from senior professionals would enrich an already strong academic offering. Increasing the frequency of student feedback collection and demonstrating visible action on that feedback would further strengthen trust between students and the institution. Overall, I am proud to be a student here and would highly recommend this university to future applicants without hesitation.
